worth_it_explanation: Campaigns bought on a DAR guaranteed on-target percentage carry a contractual commitment: the seller promises that a specified share of impressions will reach the intended demographic. When guarantee terms are not tracked alongside actual DAR results, and when end-of-campaign delivery is not reconciled against the contracted figure, a shortfall goes unclaimed and the buyer absorbs the loss. On programmatic guaranteed deals where billing is tied to in-demo impressions, the same gap means the buyer may pay for off-target delivery that the deal terms should have excluded. These checks are document reviews and DAR report reads, not system rebuilds.
intro: Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) measures the share of ad impressions that reach a campaign's intended demographic, reported as an on-target percentage. On guaranteed buys, the insertion order (IO) or deal terms specify a minimum on-target percentage the seller must deliver, with a make-good obligation if the campaign falls short. On programmatic guaranteed video deals with in-demo billing enabled, only impressions that DAR confirms as in-target are billed to the buyer. These checks confirm that guarantee terms are documented, that end-of-campaign DAR results are reconciled against the contract, and that in-demo billing figures are verified against the DAR-measured on-target count.

audit_checks.title: On-target guarantee terms are not tracked alongside DAR results, making a shortfall unclaimable
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A shortfall against a guaranteed on-target percentage entitles the buyer to a make-good. Without a record of the contracted on-target percentage alongside the actual DAR result, the buyer has no basis to assert a claim at the end of the campaign and absorbs the cost of a delivery that did not meet the agreed terms.

educate.base:
When a campaign is bought on a Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) on-target guarantee, the insertion order (IO) or deal terms commit the seller to delivering a specified share of impressions to the intended demographic, expressed as an on-target percentage. DAR defines that percentage as impressions reaching the target demographic divided by total impressions served. If the delivered on-target percentage falls below the contracted figure, the buyer is entitled to a make-good, meaning additional inventory at no charge to bring the demographic delivery up to the guaranteed level.

The make-good entitlement exists only if the buyer can demonstrate the shortfall. That requires two things to be on record at campaign end: the contracted on-target percentage and the actual DAR-measured on-target percentage for the same flight. Without both, the buyer cannot calculate the gap, cannot quantify the volume of impressions owed, and has no documented basis for a claim.

In practice, the contracted on-target percentage lives in the IO or the deal proposal. The actual result lives in the DAR report pulled from the ad server or from the Nielsen DAR interface. The check is whether anyone has placed both numbers next to each other and noted the difference. A campaign that delivered a 62% on-target rate against a 70% guarantee represents a meaningful shortfall; a campaign that delivered 74% against the same 70% guarantee had no shortfall. Neither conclusion is reachable without the comparison.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The IO or deal terms include a guaranteed on-target percentage but no post-campaign document compares the contracted figure against the DAR result
  • The DAR report for the campaign was pulled after the flight ended but was used only to report reach metrics, not to compare against the guarantee
  • The team does not have a standard process for documenting guarantee terms in a form that can be compared against DAR results at reconciliation
You may want to confirm that the contracted on-target percentage from the IO is recorded in the same place as the post-campaign DAR on-target result before the campaign closes.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a display or video campaign on a DAR-guaranteed on-target basis, the make-good process requires a documented comparison between the IO's contracted on-target percentage and the end-of-campaign DAR result. You may want to confirm that comparison exists before the campaign's final reconciliation period closes.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser buying a guaranteed demographic audience on a cost-per-thousand (CPM) deal, an undocumented shortfall means the budget was spent without delivering the audience the IO promised. You may want to confirm the contracted on-target percentage and the final DAR result are both on record and compared.

learn_more.source_title: Metrics for Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings
Activate Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings for a Line Item

investigate.intro: Locate the IO or deal terms for each DAR-guaranteed campaign, pull the final DAR on-target percentage, and confirm whether the two have been compared.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: Obtain a copy of the IO or deal proposal for the campaign. Locate the line specifying the guaranteed on-target percentage and the demographic it applies to (age range and gender).

Pull the end-of-campaign DAR report. In the ad server's reporting interface, run a Nielsen DAR report for the campaign date range and confirm the on-target percentage metric for the guaranteed demographic.

Place the contracted on-target percentage and the delivered on-target percentage side by side. Calculate the gap: if the delivered percentage is below the contracted figure, note the percentage-point shortfall and the total impression volume, which together determine the scale of any make-good owed.

Check whether a make-good request was initiated. If the delivered on-target percentage was below the contracted figure and no make-good was requested or documented, record the campaign as a shortfall with no claim on file.

evaluate.logic: For each DAR-guaranteed campaign in the review period, the practitioner records the contracted on-target percentage from the IO, the actual DAR on-target percentage from the end-of-campaign report, and whether the two were compared in a reconciliation document. A campaign with both figures recorded and compared passes. A campaign where the IO specifies a guaranteed on-target percentage but no post-campaign comparison document exists is a fail, recorded with the campaign name, the contracted figure, and the DAR result if it can be retrieved.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the contracted on-target percentage from the IO and the actual DAR result above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that DAR-guaranteed campaigns on {{site_name}} lack a documented comparison between the contracted on-target percentage and the DAR-measured result at campaign end. Without that comparison, a shortfall against the guarantee goes unclaimed, and the make-good entitlement in the IO cannot be exercised. {{voice_recommend}} recording the contracted on-target percentage from each IO alongside the post-campaign DAR on-target result, calculating any shortfall in percentage points and impression volume, and initiating a make-good request when the delivered rate falls below the contracted figure.

audit_checks.title: End-of-campaign DAR results are not reconciled against the on-target guarantee, so owed make-goods are not pursued
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A make-good owed under a DAR guarantee has a finite claim window. When the end-of-campaign DAR result is not reviewed against the contracted on-target percentage, a shortfall that entitles the buyer to additional inventory expires without being claimed, and the budget spent on off-target impressions is not recovered.

educate.base:
Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) reports are typically available at a lag of two to three days after delivery, and final campaign-level data is available after the flight ends. The on-target percentage in the final DAR report represents Nielsen's audited measurement of what share of impressions reached the contracted demographic. That figure is what make-good calculations are based on.

A make-good is additional inventory provided at no charge by the seller when the delivered on-target percentage falls below the contracted figure. The volume of impressions owed in a make-good is typically calculated as the number of on-target impressions that would have been needed to reach the guaranteed percentage, minus the number actually delivered. Make-good terms and claim deadlines are specified in the IO or deal agreement, and they vary by seller and platform.

When the post-campaign DAR report is reviewed only for reach or frequency metrics and not compared against the IO's guarantee, a shortfall can go unnoticed. The team may see a DAR on-target percentage of 61% and note it as a data point without recognizing that the IO guaranteed 70% and the gap represents a make-good entitlement. The make-good window passes, and the budget spent on the off-target 9% is not recovered.

A few signs this check applies:
  • Post-campaign DAR reports are filed or shared but not compared line-by-line against the IO's guarantee terms
  • The campaign team receives the final DAR on-target percentage but has no step in the campaign close process that flags a shortfall for follow-up
  • Historical campaign records show DAR on-target percentages below the guaranteed level with no corresponding make-good activity documented
You may want to confirm the campaign close process includes a step that compares the final DAR on-target result against the IO guarantee and initiates a follow-up if the delivered percentage falls short.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer whose media plan includes DAR-guaranteed placements across video inventory, each flight's final DAR result should be checked against the contracted on-target percentage before the campaign is closed out. You may want to confirm this review is part of the standard post-campaign process.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser buying guaranteed demographic reach, a shortfall means the audience the campaign was designed to reach was not fully delivered. You may want to confirm the final DAR on-target percentage is compared against the IO guarantee as part of campaign close, not just filed with the media report.

learn_more.source_title: Run a Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings report
Pace a line item based on Nielsen data

investigate.intro: Pull the final DAR report for each DAR-guaranteed campaign, confirm the on-target percentage, and check whether the result was compared against the IO guarantee in a reconciliation document.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: In the ad server's reporting interface, run a Nielsen DAR report for the completed campaign. Select the campaign's full flight date range and include the on-target percentage metric alongside total impressions and on-target impressions.
Retrieve the IO or deal proposal for the same campaign and note the contracted on-target percentage and the demographic it covers.
Compare the final DAR on-target percentage to the contracted figure. If the delivered percentage is below the contracted level, calculate the shortfall in percentage points.
Check the IO or deal agreement for the make-good terms and any claim deadline. Confirm whether a make-good request was filed. If not, note the claim deadline and determine whether it has passed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the final DAR on-target percentage and the contracted on-target percentage for each DAR-guaranteed campaign closed in the review period. A campaign where the final DAR result meets or exceeds the contracted percentage passes. A campaign where the DAR result falls below the contracted percentage passes only if a make-good was requested or documented as waived. A campaign where the shortfall exists and no make-good was requested or documented is a fail, recorded with the campaign name, the contracted figure, the delivered figure, and whether the make-good window has expired.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the contracted on-target percentage, the final DAR on-target result, and the make-good status above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that end-of-campaign DAR results on {{site_name}} are not reconciled against the on-target guarantee terms in the IO, leaving make-good entitlements unclaimed. When the delivered on-target percentage falls below the contracted level and no comparison is made at campaign close, the buyer has no basis to pursue the make-good that the deal terms provide. {{voice_recommend}} adding a reconciliation step to the campaign close process that compares the final DAR on-target percentage against the IO guarantee, calculates any shortfall in percentage points and impression volume, and initiates a make-good request before the claim deadline in the IO expires.

audit_checks.title: In-demo billing on programmatic guaranteed deals is not verified against the DAR-measured on-target count
audit_checks.impact_rationale: On programmatic guaranteed video deals where billing is calculated using the in-demo ratio, the buyer is charged based on the in-target percentage DAR reports for the billing period. When that ratio is not checked against the DAR report, a misconfiguration or data gap in the billing period can result in charges for off-target impressions that the deal terms excluded from billing.

educate.base:
On programmatic guaranteed video deals where in-demo billing is enabled, the buyer is charged based on total impressions delivered multiplied by the in-target ratio that Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings (DAR) reports for the billing period. For example, if 10 million impressions were delivered and DAR reports a 65% on-target ratio for that period, the billing calculation applies the agreed in-target cost per thousand (CPM) to 6.5 million impressions rather than all 10 million. The buyer is not billed for the 3.5 million impressions that reached outside the target demographic.

This billing model depends on DAR data arriving on schedule and reflecting the agreed demographic accurately. When DAR data is delayed, when the deal's target demographic was entered incorrectly, or when the in-target ratio used in billing differs from what the DAR report shows for the same period, the buyer may be charged for a different impression volume than the deal terms intended. The billing formula is documented in the platform's line item configuration, but the actual ratio applied each month is drawn from Nielsen's data delivery.

Verification means pulling the DAR on-target percentage for the billing period from the DAR report and confirming it matches the in-target ratio applied in the invoice or billing summary for that period. A material discrepancy between the two, beyond the rounding the billing formula introduces, points to a configuration or data issue worth investigating with the seller or platform before the billing is finalized.

A few signs this check applies:
  • The programmatic guaranteed deal is configured with in-demo billing enabled but the buyer has not confirmed the DAR on-target ratio used in billing against the DAR report for the same period
  • The invoice or billing summary for a programmatic guaranteed deal references an in-target impression count but the team has not cross-referenced it against the DAR report
  • A programmatic guaranteed deal ran during a month where DAR data was delayed past the billing cycle cutoff, and the buyer did not confirm how the billing ratio was handled for that period
You may want to confirm the in-target ratio applied in each billing period's calculation is consistent with the DAR on-target percentage the report shows for that same period.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a programmatic guaranteed video campaign with in-demo billing, each month's billing calculation applies the DAR in-target ratio to total impressions. You may want to confirm the ratio used in billing matches the DAR on-target percentage for the same period before approving the invoice.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser on a programmatic guaranteed deal with in-demo billing, the billing formula means the effective CPM on in-target impressions is fixed and off-target impressions are excluded. You may want to confirm the DAR on-target ratio applied in billing aligns with the DAR report for the relevant billing period.

learn_more.source_title: Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings measurement features
Measure Demographic Targeting Success with Nielsen Digital Ad Ratings

investigate.intro: Confirm whether in-demo billing is enabled on the programmatic guaranteed deal, pull the DAR on-target percentage for the billing period, and compare it against the in-target ratio applied in the billing summary.

nielsen_dar.label: Nielsen DAR
steps: Retrieve the programmatic guaranteed deal's configuration and confirm whether in-demo billing is enabled. In Google Ad Manager, look for the 'In-target CPM' cost type and the 'In-target impressions' unit type on the line item. In Microsoft Invest (Xandr), check whether the 'Audience Demo' toggle is enabled and whether an 'On Target % Goal' is set.
Pull the DAR report for the billing period in question. Include the on-target percentage and total impression count for the deal's target demographic (age range and gender).
Obtain the invoice or billing summary for the same period. Locate the in-target impression count or in-target ratio applied in the billing calculation.

Compare the DAR on-target percentage from the report against the in-target ratio in the billing summary. Note any discrepancy beyond the rounding the billing formula introduces, and flag it for follow-up with the seller or platform if material.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the DAR on-target percentage from the report for the billing period and the in-target ratio applied in the billing summary for the same period. A billing period where the two figures are consistent, within rounding, passes. A billing period where the in-target ratio used in billing differs materially from the DAR on-target percentage in the report is a fail, recorded with the deal name, the billing period, the DAR on-target percentage, and the ratio applied in billing.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the DAR on-target percentage from the report and the in-target ratio applied in billing above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that in-demo billing on programmatic guaranteed video deals on {{site_name}} is not verified against the DAR-measured on-target percentage for the corresponding billing period. When the in-target ratio applied in billing differs from the DAR report, the buyer may be charged for a different impression volume than the deal terms intended. {{voice_recommend}} pulling the DAR on-target percentage for each billing period and comparing it against the in-target ratio in the billing summary before approving invoices, and flagging any material discrepancy to the seller or platform for reconciliation.
